publicationStmt.p: Transcribed from Bodleian Library, Lovelace Byron collection, Box 166, folios 200-201. Transcription by Lisa Gold (2022). TEI conversion for Sextant pipeline, 2026.

div.p: Dearest W. Last night I was suddenly taken unwell , & very much indeed so. I am ill with it today, quite in my old way . Dr L-- has been with me, & will come again tomorrow.
I suppose there is no hope of any cessation; since
everything now seems to be arriving regularly.
But as Dr L-- observes, it is a great misfortune, & what will tend to prevent my success in life with everything I may undertake. I am glad you are not in Town; for I am half distracted today.--
I do not see how with my particular constitution, I can ever
do any good in the world. It seems to be a physical impossibility for me ever to carry on anything continuously .

The objects most liked at one time, may be at any moment hated. For instance I detest how both my Harp & my Studies. And dispite the utmost exertions & resolutions on my part, I am forced to own that utter fruitlessness of all hopes of such continuous

attention to any subject whatever, as could ensure any great ultimate success. So it is I fear. I am one of those genius's who will merely run to grass; owing to my unfortunate physical termperament.--
Pray don't be angry with me.
